How It Works Security & Privacy Free Promise FAQ For Law Firms Start Free Account

For family law attorneys, mediators, and evaluators

Stop reading months of messages.
Start reviewing findings.

Liaison.legal's AI Forensic Auditor cross-references a client's communication record against the court order, flags potential deviations, and links every finding to the message behind it — so your team reviews a draft instead of assembling one.

Request Firm Access → See how the audit works

Liaison.legal is a co-parenting communication platform with an attorney-facing forensic audit. Any firm whose client uses Liaison.legal gets free, view-only access to that client's verified record. When a matter calls for it, the AI Forensic Auditor compares the communication history against the uploaded court order, surfaces potential behavioral and financial deviations, grounds each one in a specific message, and assembles a court-ready Declaration of Non-Compliance for an attorney to review, confirm, and sign. Firms pay per matter; parents never pay.

The least billable hours in the building

Somewhere on your team, someone is reading six months of co-parenting messages to find the eight that matter. It's slow, it's a cost center, and it's the worst use of a paralegal's day. Meanwhile the evidence that wins the motion is buried in a thread no one has time to read end to end.

A neutral auditor, not a coach

Liaison.legal reads the record so your team doesn't have to:

→
Ingests the court order — Upload the judgment or parenting plan.
→
Extracts the rules — Pick-up times, locations, notice periods, exchange terms.
→
Cross-references the communication — Every message, against every rule.
→
Flags potential deviations — Behavioral and financial, with severity and frequency.
→
Grounds every finding — Each one hyperlinked to the exact message that supports it. No ungrounded claims.
→
Assembles the Declaration — A court-ready Declaration of Non-Compliance, drafted for your review.

The auditor identifies potential deviations in factual, cross-referenced terms. It does not draw legal conclusions and it does not advise. The interpretation — and the signature — stay with the attorney.

Your review is the work product

No finding leaves the system until an attorney reviews and confirms it. That step is deliberate. It's the safeguard against unauthorized practice of law, and it's what makes the finished Declaration your firm's billable work product — a deliverable added, not hours subtracted.

Reallocate capacity to advocacy

The audit automates the work that never billed well anyway. The hours you save go back into higher-margin advocacy, the Declaration becomes a billable deliverable, and better-grounded evidence wins more matters. The precedent is e-discovery: once the tools existed, reading by hand stopped being defensible. Sell win-rate and capacity — not "time saved."

How firm pricing works

Free view-only on-ramp

Read access to any client's record who already uses Liaison.legal, at no cost.

Per-matter audit & Declaration

Charged when the auditor is activated and a Declaration is produced. Priced to sit below the paralegal assembly hours it replaces, and pass-through-able as a client disbursement.

Volume subscription

For firms running enough matters that a flat license beats per-matter, bundling white-label branding.

Final per-matter pricing is being set with our launch cohort. Request access to discuss your firm's volume.

Your portal, your brand

The platform can carry your firm's branding, so the record your client builds lives inside your proprietary client portal. The firm is the hero; we're the engine.

See it on a real matter.

We'll walk your team through a live audit on a sample case.

We'll be in touch within one business day.

Thank you — we'll be in touch within one business day to schedule your walk-through.

Liaison.legal

Tamper-evident co-parenting communication. Free for parents, forever.

Not a law firm. Does not provide legal advice.

Liaison.legal is a record-keeping tool, not an emergency service. If you or your child are in immediate danger, call 911 or your local emergency number.
